mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic strength and rising corporate revenues, leading to positive equity market revisions as earnings expectations are revised upwards.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a widely watched coincident indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates an abrupt slowdown in consumer spending, a primary driver of GDP, leading to negative revisions of corporate earnings forecasts and lower equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Sharp consumer spending drops often precede recessions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This creates uncertainty and expectations of future aggressive tightening, increasing the equity risk premium and negatively impacting valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:The 1970s stagflation period showed this dynamic to be negative for equities.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns with an accuracy greater than a 55% baseline during periods matching the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is high (e.g., >6%), a decrease signals cyclical recovery and improving demand without immediate wage-push inflation threats, boosting corporate profit expectations and equity prices. The signal's direction inverts at low unemployment levels.
evidence_refs: fact:The snapshot UNRATE of 9.0% is in this high unemployment regime.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6%, the correlation between monthly changes in UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and business activity, translating into higher expected corporate earnings and supporting higher equity valuations over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a rolling 24-month correlation with 5-day forward returns of less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent downturn in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad-based weakening of consumer balance sheets and confidence. This interaction points to a likely recession, leading to significant negative revisions in earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in HOUST < 0 AND 3-month change in RSAFS < 0
evidence_refs: fact:Housing and consumption are key cyclical pillars of the US economy.
failure_condition: In periods where both series show negative 3-month momentum, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, it signals severe underlying economic weakness requiring extraordinary accommodation. This fragility makes equities vulnerable to negative shocks, leading to poor short-term returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The post-2008 ZIRP era was characterized by periodic risk-off shocks.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is not statistically significantly lower than when FEDFUNDS > 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) concurrent with decelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates the economy is losing momentum and entering a downturn. This combination precedes sharp declines in corporate earnings and equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in UNRATE > 0.3pp AND QoQ % change in GDPC1 is lower than the previous quarter's
evidence_refs: fact:This pattern, known as the Sahm Rule precursor, is a reliable recession indicator.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has positive 60-day forward returns in over 50% of the quarters where the trigger condition is met.
